title: Streaming Service Validation Cascade with Incremental Authorization Testing
narrative: Fraudster conducts systematic validation of compromised card credentials through sequential streaming service trial signups across Netflix, Hulu, Disney+, Spotify, and HBO Max within a 45-90 minute window. Initial transactions start with $0.99-$1.99 authorization holds to confirm card validity, followed by immediate $7.99-$14.99 full subscription charges once validation succeeds. All transactions originate from residential IP (dist1 < 50km from cardholder billing) but use temporary email domain (P_emaildomain = 'guerrillamail.com' or 'tempmail.net') inconsistent with account history. Pattern shows precise MCC 5815 (Digital Goods - Media) clustering with zero failed authorizations, suggesting batch-testing of stolen credentials. Fraudster deliberately spaces transactions 8-12 minutes apart to evade basic velocity triggers while maintaining momentum before card is blocked.
indicators: 5-7 transactions to MCC 5815 merchants within 45-90 minute window, all approved
TransactionAmt progression: 2-3 charges $0.99-$1.99, followed by 3-4 charges $7.99-$14.99
P_emaildomain matches disposable email services (guerrillamail, tempmail, 10minutemail) with account age < 1 hour
card1_merchant_diversity_1h >= 5 distinct streaming merchants, card1_mcc5815_count_24h = 0 historically
dist1 < 50km (IP geolocation near billing addr1) but R_emaildomain != historical email domain
Uniform 8-12 minute spacing between transactions (card1_txn_time_delta_mean = 600 seconds)
ProductCD = 'C' (card-not-present) with 100% approval rate and zero CVV/AVS mismatches
summary: Multiple streaming service signups (Netflix, Hulu, Spotify) occur within 45-90 minutes using the same card, starting with small test charges under $2 that immediately escalate to full subscriptions of $8-15, all perfectly approved with 8-12 minute gaps between transactions and temporary email addresses.
reasoning: The fraudster is systematically testing a stolen card across different services to validate it works before maximizing the theft, using disposable emails and precise timing to avoid fraud detection while the card is still active.
indicator_explanations: Multiple streaming service signups cluster together in a single session
Transaction amounts escalate from trial tiers to full subscription prices
Email address comes from throwaway service created moments before attacks
Card hits several streaming platforms it has never used before
Location matches billing address but email domain suddenly changes
Transactions follow mechanical timing pattern like automated testing script
All remote purchases sail through without any verification failures
